methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $375M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Quantinuum
private.rationale: Same sector (Quantum Computing), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B

data.narrative:
Photonic Inc is a Series E quantum computing company that has raised $375M CAD to date. The sector includes 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 7 private peers, notably Quantinuum, IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Only one valuation method proved applicable. The stage-progression approach uses the $375M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement, yielding $525M. A peer-median approach could not be deployed due to insufficient narrow comparables: while 7 same-sector peers exist, filtering for matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier produced fewer than 3 datapoints. Broadening to all-sector medians would introduce excessive noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $446M (low), $525M (median), to $604M (high), anchored entirely to stage-progression mechanics. The key uncertainty is the absence of sufficiently granular peer data within quantum computing at comparable stages and valuations. This constraint limits conviction in the range; reliance on a single method means sensitivity to the 1.4x multiplier assumption. Any future valuation update should prioritize acquisition of additional same-stage or adjacent-stage quantum computing transactions to cross-validate via peer multiples.
